title: Implement backpressure responses and connection lifecycle state machine
purpose: Deliver the backpressure response vocabulary (Accept, Defer, Reject) per ADR-004 and the connection lifecycle state machine (connecting, connected, reconnecting, disconnected) per ADR-008. Backpressure is surfaced as typed responses on every publish operation -- the SDK never silently consumes pressure signals (constraint CN6). The connection state machine provides the foundation for automatic reconnection with exponential backoff and jitter (constraint CN7), and subscription state recovery from the last acknowledged sequence.
task: Create pressure.rs for backpressure types, and the connection/ module with mod.rs, lifecycle.rs, and recovery.rs. PressureResponse is an enum returned from publish operations. The connection state machine is a typed state enum with defined transitions. Recovery tracks per-subscription sequence numbers for replay on reconnect. IN SCOPE: backpressure types, connection state machine, reconnection strategy, subscription recovery. OUT OF SCOPE: connection pooling (SDK-004), concrete transport (SDK-004), channel/conversation logic (SDK-002).

requirements.title: Define PressureResponse enum with Accept, Defer, and Reject variants
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a PressureResponse enum with three variants: Accept (message delivered), Defer { delay: Duration } (message buffered, estimated delay provided), and Reject { reason: String } (consumer overwhelmed, message shed). WHEN a publish operation completes, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a PressureResponse alongside the result. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT silently convert Defer or Reject to Accept.
acceptance: PressureResponse enum exists with exactly three variants: Accept, Defer, Reject
Defer carries a Duration field indicating estimated delay
Reject carries a reason String describing why the message was shed
PressureResponse derives Debug, Clone, and PartialEq
PressureResponse is re-exported from the crate root

files: crates/liminal-sdk/src/lib.rs:1-13 — crate root uses no_std + alloc, declares modules, and re-exports public SDK types; add pressure module and PressureResponse export here.

crates/liminal-sdk/src/channel.rs:55-66 — ChannelHandle::publish currently returns Result<(), SdkError>; this is the existing publish surface that will eventually need Result<PressureResponse, SdkError> or an equivalent alongside-result shape.

crates/liminal-sdk/src/conversation.rs:97-105 — ConversationHandle::send is another outbound message API currently returning Result<(), SdkError>; brief says all publish operations, so avoid broadening conversation send unless intentionally scoped by the brief boundaries.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:43-48,248-266 — core protocol already has Accept, Defer, Reject frame types and payload fields: credit, retry_after_ms, and optional reject message.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/error.rs:38-43 — SdkError has an existing Backpressure error variant, but SDK-003 wants pressure as a successful typed response, not an error that hides Defer/Reject semantics.
context: The SDK crate is no_std under non-std builds and imports alloc at crate root; use core::time::Duration and alloc::string::String in the new pressure module.
Public SDK types consistently derive Debug, Clone, PartialEq where values are comparable; docs use Rustdoc comments on every public item and #[must_use] on constructors/accessors.

ChannelHandle::publish is synchronous today and returns Result<(), SdkError>, so changing it to return PressureResponse is a public trait-breaking change but is required by the brief acceptance and C7.
Do not model Defer/Reject as SdkError::Backpressure; CN6 says every pressure signal reaches the app and the requirement says not to silently convert Defer or Reject to Accept.
scout.approach: Create crates/liminal-sdk/src/pressure.rs with `use alloc::string::String; use core::time::Duration;` and a `#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]` or at least `Debug, Clone, PartialEq` enum `PressureResponse { Accept, Defer { delay: Duration }, Reject { reason: String } }`. Add `pub mod pressure;` and `pub use pressure::PressureResponse;` to lib.rs. Update ChannelHandle::publish to return `Result<PressureResponse, SdkError>` so a successful publish reports Accept/Defer/Reject directly; if the implementer considers request_reply a publish-like operation, document why it remains unchanged or return pressure in a wrapper only if the wider API design demands it.
scout.notes: core::time::Duration is available without std, but adding rand/getrandom just for this requirement would violate the minimal no_std API surface and belongs more to R3. Watch Clippy pedantic: if the enum carries a Duration field named `delay`, Rustdoc should explain estimated delay and reject reason clearly.

files_changed.note: Defines PressureResponse with Accept, Defer { delay: Duration }, and Reject { reason: String } using no_std-compatible alloc/core types.
ChannelHandle::publish now returns Result<PressureResponse, SdkError> and documents accept/defer/reject visibility.
Adds pressure module declaration and re-exports PressureResponse from the crate root.

dev.how: Added the public liminal_sdk::pressure::PressureResponse enum deriving Debug, Clone, PartialEq, and Eq, with Duration-backed Defer and String-backed Reject payloads. Updated the SDK publish surface to return the typed pressure response directly so Defer and Reject remain application-visible successful pressure signals rather than being converted to Accept or hidden as errors; conversation send/request-reply were left unchanged because they are not the SDK publish operation targeted by C7 and broader channel/conversation logic is out of scope.

checklist.note: PressureResponse exists in liminal_sdk::pressure, is re-exported at the crate root, and ChannelHandle::publish returns Result<PressureResponse, SdkError>.

stories.note: Rust callers receive typed Accept/Defer/Reject signals from publish and can implement application-level flow control.

acceptance.criterion: PressureResponse enum exists with exactly three variants: Accept, Defer, Reject
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/pressure.rs:10-22 defines PressureResponse with only Accept, Defer, and Reject variants.

acceptance.criterion: Defer carries a Duration field indicating estimated delay
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/pressure.rs:14-17 defines Defer { delay: Duration } with core::time::Duration imported at pressure.rs:2.

acceptance.criterion: Reject carries a reason String describing why the message was shed
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/pressure.rs:19-22 defines Reject { reason: String } with alloc::string::String imported at pressure.rs:1.

acceptance.criterion: PressureResponse derives Debug, Clone, and PartialEq
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/pressure.rs:9 derives Clone, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq.

acceptance.criterion: PressureResponse is re-exported from the crate root
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/lib.rs:9 declares the pressure module and lib.rs:19 re-exports PressureResponse from the crate root.

requirements.title: Implement connection lifecycle state machine with observable transitions
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a ConnectionState enum with four states: Connecting, Connected, Reconnecting { attempt: u32 }, and Disconnected { reason: DisconnectReason }. WHEN a state transition occurs, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a ConnectionEvent to all registered observers. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow invalid transitions (e.g. Disconnected to Connected without passing through Connecting). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose the state machine's internal transition logic to callers.
acceptance: ConnectionState enum has exactly four variants: Connecting, Connected, Reconnecting, Disconnected
Reconnecting carries an attempt counter
Disconnected carries a DisconnectReason enum (Normal, Error, Timeout)
A ConnectionEvents stream is available for observing state transitions
Transition from Disconnected directly to Connected is rejected at compile time or returns an error

files: crates/liminal-sdk/src/lib.rs:5-13 — add `pub mod connection;` and re-export connection public types from the crate root if they are intended top-level SDK API.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/mod.rs:1-10 — new file should contain only `pub mod lifecycle;`, `pub mod recovery;` when R4 lands, and `pub use ...`; CN3 forbids any logic here.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:1-220 — new file for ConnectionState, DisconnectReason, ConnectionEvent, ConnectionEvents, and the private/internal state machine transition API.

crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:3-16,18-80,81-133 — core protocol lifecycle provides a useful pattern: state enum, transition methods returning Result, and lifecycle action type, but its states are wire-handshake states, not the SDK remote lifecycle states.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/error.rs:8-15 — SdkError::Connection exists and can carry invalid transition descriptions if you do not add a more specific public transition error.
context: There is currently no SDK connection module; the SDK has only channel, conversation, error, and types modules.

The core protocol ConnectionState has Connecting/Authenticating/Active/Closing/Closed; do not reuse it directly for SDK lifecycle because SDK requirement demands exactly Connecting, Connected, Reconnecting { attempt }, Disconnected { reason }.

Existing public traits expose stream associated types with futures_core::Stream; a `ConnectionEvents` stream can be a trait alias-like public type parameter pattern or a concrete no_std-compatible receiver wrapper, but avoid introducing tokio/broadcast/std sync into this crate.
Invalid transition can be represented by an error-returning state-machine method; compile-time typestate is possible but overkill and would complicate observer emission.
scout.approach: Implement lifecycle.rs around a small public value model plus an internal transition owner. Define `ConnectionState`, `DisconnectReason`, and `ConnectionEvent { previous: ConnectionState, current: ConnectionState }` (or similarly explicit fields) with Debug/Clone/PartialEq/Eq. Define an opaque `ConnectionLifecycle<E>` or `ConnectionLifecycle` that owns current state and an event sink/observer list supplied generically, exposes `state()` and specific transition methods (`connect`, `connected`, `reconnect`, `disconnect`) that validate allowed edges, emit a ConnectionEvent after state changes, and return `Result<(), SdkError>` for invalid edges such as Disconnected -> Connected. For `ConnectionEvents`, prefer a public trait/associated stream abstraction or a lightweight `type ConnectionEvents<S> = S`-style pattern constrained by `Stream<Item = ConnectionEvent>` rather than committing to a concrete async runtime.
scout.notes: The brief says transition logic must not be exposed to callers, so avoid a public `transition_to(ConnectionState)` API. Also avoid putting state-machine logic in connection/mod.rs; CN3 says mod.rs is declarations/re-exports only.

files_changed.note: Logic-free module declaration and re-export file for connection lifecycle and recovery APIs.
Defines ConnectionState, DisconnectReason, ConnectionEvent, ConnectionEvents, and ConnectionLifecycle with validated transition methods and observer emission.
Contains lifecycle transition, observer, and reconnect-attempt tests split out to keep lifecycle.rs below the file-size boundary.
Adds connection module declaration and re-exports lifecycle public types.

dev.how: Implemented the SDK lifecycle model with exactly Connecting, Connected, Reconnecting { attempt }, and Disconnected { reason } states plus Normal/Error/Timeout disconnect reasons. ConnectionLifecycle owns the state and exposes specific transition methods instead of a public transition_to API; invalid edges such as Disconnected -> Connected return SdkError, and each successful transition emits a ConnectionEvent to all registered observers. A generic ConnectionEvents<S> stream wrapper is available for runtime-specific event streams without adding a concrete async runtime dependency.

checklist.note: Connection lifecycle state machine and observable transition events are implemented with invalid transition rejection.

stories.note: The lifecycle state machine provides the observable reconnect state foundation needed for automatic reconnection and recovery.

acceptance.criterion: ConnectionState enum has exactly four variants: Connecting, Connected, Reconnecting, Disconnected
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:18-33 defines exactly Connecting, Connected, Reconnecting, and Disconnected.

acceptance.criterion: Reconnecting carries an attempt counter
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:23-27 defines Reconnecting { attempt: u32 }.

acceptance.criterion: Disconnected carries a DisconnectReason enum (Normal, Error, Timeout)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:28-43 defines Disconnected { reason: DisconnectReason } and DisconnectReason variants Normal, Error, Timeout.

acceptance.criterion: A ConnectionEvents stream is available for observing state transitions
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:63-115 defines ConnectionEvents<S> and implements Stream<Item = ConnectionEvent>; lifecycle observers are registered at lifecycle.rs:243-245 and emitted at lifecycle.rs:328-334.

acceptance.criterion: Transition from Disconnected directly to Connected is rejected at compile time or returns an error
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:268-276 allows connected() only from Connecting or Reconnecting and returns invalid_transition otherwise; test connection::lifecycle::tests::disconnected_to_connected_is_rejected at lifecycle/tests.rs:28-38 asserts the error path.

requirements.title: Implement exponential backoff with jitter for reconnection
requirements.spec: WHEN the connection transitions to Reconnecting, THE SYSTEM SHALL compute the next retry delay as min(base_delay * 2^attempt, max_delay) plus a random jitter factor. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use fixed-interval retry (constraint CN7). THE SYSTEM SHALL accept configurable base_delay and max_delay parameters. THE SYSTEM SHALL reset the attempt counter to zero when a connection is successfully established.
acceptance: Reconnection delay for attempt N equals min(base_delay * 2^N, max_delay) + jitter
Jitter is a random value between 0 and 50% of the computed delay
base_delay and max_delay are configurable via a ReconnectConfig struct
After successful connection, the next reconnection attempt starts at attempt 0
No code path uses a fixed retry interval

files: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:1-240 — add ReconnectConfig and delay computation near the Reconnecting transition/state machine code.
crates/liminal-sdk/Cargo.toml:11-18 — SDK dependencies are futures-core, serde, thiserror with no_std defaults; any jitter dependency must preserve no_std+alloc/WASM compatibility or be avoided.
Cargo.toml:19-28 — workspace denies unsafe, unwrap, expect, panic and denies Clippy all/pedantic, so saturating/checked math and explicit errors are required.
docs/design/sdk/design.json:252-265 — CN4/CN5/CN6/CN7: public types Debug, no_std+alloc, pressure explicit, exponential backoff with jitter only.

crates/liminal/src/protocol/lifecycle.rs:124-133 — core lifecycle uses pure deterministic transition helpers; SDK backoff calculation should be similarly testable and side-effect-free except the jitter source.
context: There is no existing rand dependency in liminal-sdk; adding rand can easily pull std unless configured carefully, so inject jitter as a trait/function parameter or store a small no_std RNG abstraction instead of hard-coding thread_rng.
Duration arithmetic can overflow when multiplying base_delay by 2^attempt; use checked/saturating arithmetic and cap at max_delay before applying jitter.

Requirement says attempt N is `min(base_delay * 2^N, max_delay) + jitter`, with jitter from 0% to 50% of computed capped delay; tests should assert the base range, not one deterministic random value unless injecting deterministic jitter.
Successful connection must reset the next reconnection attempt to 0; that belongs in the lifecycle transition from Connecting/Reconnecting to Connected, not in the delay calculator only.
scout.approach: Add `ReconnectConfig { base_delay: Duration, max_delay: Duration }` with a constructor and Default if appropriate. Implement a pure helper such as `base_delay_for_attempt(attempt)` that computes the capped exponential delay with checked/saturating doubling, plus `retry_delay(attempt, jitter: impl JitterSource)` or `retry_delay_with_jitter_fraction(attempt, jitter_numerator)` so tests can deterministically cover 0% and 50%. Wire the lifecycle's transition to Reconnecting to increment/track attempts and expose the computed delay/action, and ensure any transition to Connected resets the stored attempt counter so a later disruption starts at attempt 0.
scout.notes: Do not implement a fixed interval fallback when random jitter is unavailable; if randomness cannot be sourced in no_std, require injection from the concrete transport/client layer. Be careful that adding jitter after max cap can exceed max_delay; that matches the spec wording, but document it because some readers expect max to cap total delay.

files_changed.note: Adds ReconnectConfig, ReconnectJitter, capped exponential delay calculation, jitter validation, and reconnect attempt reset behavior.
Tests exponential delay plus jitter bounds and successful-connection attempt reset.
Re-exports ReconnectConfig and ReconnectJitter from the crate root.

dev.how: Added configurable ReconnectConfig { base_delay, max_delay } and a no_std-friendly injected ReconnectJitter trait. Delay computation uses min(base_delay * 2^attempt, max_delay) as the capped component and adds validated jitter up to 50% of that capped component; overflow saturates safely rather than wrapping. ConnectionLifecycle::reconnect returns the computed delay and advances the zero-based attempt counter, while any successful connected() transition resets the next reconnect attempt to zero.

checklist.note: Reconnection delay uses configurable exponential backoff with injected jitter and no fixed-interval retry path.

stories.note: Reconnect attempts now carry attempt state and computed retry delay suitable for automatic reconnection.

acceptance.criterion: Reconnection delay for attempt N equals min(base_delay * 2^N, max_delay) + jitter
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:136-149 computes the capped exponential component with saturating multiplication and max cap; lifecycle.rs:175-189 adds validated jitter; test connection::lifecycle::tests::reconnect_delay_uses_exponential_backoff_and_jitter covers attempts 0..=10 at lifecycle/tests.rs:60-70.

acceptance.criterion: Jitter is a random value between 0 and 50% of the computed delay
acceptance.evidence: ReconnectJitter is the injected random jitter source at crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:199-206; lifecycle.rs:180-186 rejects jitter greater than 50% of capped delay; lifecycle/tests.rs:80-84 asserts an over-limit jitter errors.

acceptance.criterion: base_delay and max_delay are configurable via a ReconnectConfig struct
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:117-124 defines public ReconnectConfig { base_delay, max_delay }, with constructor at lifecycle.rs:126-134.

acceptance.criterion: After successful connection, the next reconnection attempt starts at attempt 0
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:268-272 resets next_reconnect_attempt to 0 in connected(); test connection::lifecycle::tests::successful_connection_resets_reconnect_attempts at lifecycle/tests.rs:95-127 verifies attempt resets to 0 after reconnecting then connected().

acceptance.criterion: No code path uses a fixed retry interval
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:289-300 routes reconnect() through reconnect_config.retry_delay() for every reconnect transition; retry_delay uses capped exponential + jitter at lifecycle.rs:157-163. Source search for retry/fixed/interval found no separate fixed-interval retry loop in crates/liminal-sdk/src.

issues: ReconnectConfig::capped_delay originally used a left-shifted u128 value as the exponential component; for very large base_delay values this could truncate before applying max_delay instead of saturating to the configured cap.

The measured verification claimed exponential backoff attempts 0 through 10 were unit-tested, but the actual test coverage only asserted a subset before the harden pass; this divergence was treated as signal and fixed.
fixes: Changed ReconnectConfig::capped_delay to compute a checked shift multiplier and use saturating_mul before min(max_delay), preventing overflow/truncation before capping (crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle.rs:146-148).

Expanded connection::lifecycle::tests::reconnect_delay_uses_exponential_backoff_and_jitter to loop over attempts 0_u32..=10 and assert capped delay plus jitter (crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle/tests.rs:60-70).
Added connection::lifecycle::tests::reconnect_delay_saturates_to_max_delay_on_large_attempts to lock the overflow-saturation behavior (crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/lifecycle/tests.rs:88-93).

requirements.title: Implement subscription state recovery from last acknowledged sequence
requirements.spec: WHEN the connection transitions from Reconnecting to Connected, THE SYSTEM SHALL replay each active subscription from its last acknowledged sequence number. THE SYSTEM SHALL track per-subscription sequence numbers as messages are acknowledged. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT request full replay from sequence zero if a prior acknowledged position exists.
acceptance: SubscriptionRecovery struct tracks a map of subscription_id to last_acknowledged_sequence
On reconnect, each subscription's resume request includes its last acknowledged sequence
A subscription with no prior acknowledgement starts from sequence zero
A subscription with acknowledged sequence N resumes from sequence N+1
Recovery state is cleared when a subscription is explicitly unsubscribed

files: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/recovery.rs:1-180 — new file for SubscriptionRecovery, subscription identifier type, resume request type, and ack/unsubscribe APIs.
crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/mod.rs:1-12 — re-export recovery types from the connection module while keeping mod.rs logic-free.

crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:171-197 — wire subscribe/unsubscribe frames currently carry subscription_id; recovery resume requests should model subscription IDs without implementing transport frames.
crates/liminal/src/durability/cursor.rs:5-18,34-52,67-101 — core durability cursor convention: absent stored offset starts at 0, checkpoint refuses regression, and current offset is explicit.

crates/liminal/src/durability/recovery.rs:84-100,103-134 — existing recovery code resumes from stored cursor/sequence and uses checked_add for next sequence, which is the important overflow/gotcha pattern for SDK recovery too.
context: liminal-sdk cannot use std::collections::HashMap in no_std+alloc; use alloc::collections::BTreeMap or a Vec-backed map unless a no_std hash map dependency is intentionally added.

Existing wire protocol subscription IDs are u64, so a simple `SubscriptionId(u64)` newtype with Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq/Ord/Hash is likely enough; if channel names are needed later, keep them out of this brief.
Acceptance says no prior acknowledgement starts from sequence zero, and acknowledged N resumes from N+1; use checked_add to avoid u64::MAX overflow instead of wrapping to zero.
Clearing recovery state on explicit unsubscribe is a local state mutation only; do not implement actual unsubscribe transport, connection pooling, or channel logic.
scout.approach: Create recovery.rs with a public `SubscriptionRecovery` owning `BTreeMap<SubscriptionId, u64>` for last acknowledged sequences. Add methods like `acknowledge(subscription_id, sequence)`, `resume_sequence(subscription_id) -> Result<u64, SdkError>` returning 0 if absent and `last + 1` if present, `resume_requests()` returning a Vec of `{ subscription_id, from_sequence }`, and `unsubscribe(subscription_id)` removing the entry. To support active subscriptions with no acks, either add `track_subscription(subscription_id)` with no stored sequence or make reconnect accept an active subscription iterator and combine it with the ack map; otherwise `resume_requests()` cannot include never-acknowledged active subscriptions as required.
scout.notes: The phrase “tracks a map of subscription_id to last_acknowledged_sequence” does not by itself track active subscriptions with no acknowledgements; the implementer needs an active set or a reconnect method that is passed active IDs. Also avoid using HashMap from std and avoid silently wrapping N+1 on u64 overflow.

files_changed.note: Defines SubscriptionRecovery, SubscriptionId, ResumeRequest, ack tracking, resume sequence generation, transition-aware resume request generation, and unsubscribe cleanup.
Re-exports recovery public types from the crate root.

dev.how: Implemented SubscriptionRecovery with an alloc::collections::BTreeMap<SubscriptionId, u64> for last acknowledged sequences and a no_std-compatible active subscription set. Active subscriptions with no acknowledgement resume from sequence zero; acknowledged sequence N resumes from N+1 using checked_add to avoid wrapping; explicit unsubscribe removes active and acknowledged state. A reconnect-to-connected ConnectionEvent can be passed to resume_requests_for_transition to produce per-subscription ResumeRequest values only for recovery transitions, with non-recovery transitions returning no requests.

checklist.note: Recovery tracks last acknowledged sequence numbers and generates resume requests from the correct sequence after reconnect.

stories.note: Tracked subscriptions can be replayed from the last acknowledged position when reconnection completes.

acceptance.criterion: SubscriptionRecovery struct tracks a map of subscription_id to last_acknowledged_sequence
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/recovery.rs:55-60 defines SubscriptionRecovery with acknowledged: BTreeMap<SubscriptionId, u64> and active subscription tracking.

acceptance.criterion: On reconnect, each subscription's resume request includes its last acknowledged sequence
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/recovery.rs:37-42 defines ResumeRequest with subscription_id and from_sequence; recovery.rs:130-138 produces requests only for Reconnecting -> Connected transitions and recovery.rs:143-150 builds one request per active subscription from tracked sequence state.

acceptance.criterion: A subscription with no prior acknowledgement starts from sequence zero
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/recovery.rs:100-105 returns Ok(0) when no acknowledgement exists; test connection::recovery::tests::subscriptions_without_acknowledgement_resume_from_zero verifies 0 at recovery.rs:174-185.

acceptance.criterion: A subscription with acknowledged sequence N resumes from sequence N+1
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/recovery.rs:100-111 uses checked_add(1); test connection::recovery::tests::acknowledged_subscriptions_resume_after_last_sequence verifies ack 41 resumes at 42 at recovery.rs:189-204, and reconnect recovery ack 5 -> 6 at recovery.rs:222-236.

acceptance.criterion: Recovery state is cleared when a subscription is explicitly unsubscribed
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-sdk/src/connection/recovery.rs:156-160 removes acknowledged state and active tracking; test connection::recovery::tests::unsubscribe_clears_recovery_state verifies cleanup at recovery.rs:208-219.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement connection pooling (deferred to SDK-004).
SHALL NOT implement concrete wire protocol transport.
SHALL NOT implement channel or conversation logic.
SHALL NOT silently consume, buffer, or retry on backpressure signals.
SHALL NOT use fixed-interval retry for reconnection.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal-sdk -- -D warnings passes with no diagnostics
cargo fmt -p liminal-sdk --check reports no formatting issues
Unit tests cover all four connection state transitions
Unit tests verify exponential backoff delay computation for attempts 0 through 10
Unit tests verify subscription recovery sequence tracking
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
